title: quercus × avellaniformis subsp. morisii
intro: Quercus × avellaniformis subsp. morisii, also known as the hybrid of sessile oak and pedunculate oak, is a subspecies of deciduous tree that is native to the Italian Alps. The bark and leaves of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Quercus × avellaniformis subsp. morisii include reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and aiding in digestion, as well as having antioxidant and antimicrobial effects. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal and health benefits.
